how many times has brazil won the world.answer: Brazil has won the World Cup **five times**, making them the most successful World Cup team in history. They are also the only nation to have played in every World Cup tournament to date. Brazil were the first team to win the World Cup for the third time (1970), the fourth time (1994), and the fifth time (2002).

what are the rules for leg before wicket.answer:
# Leg Before Wicket (LBW) Rules

According to cricket law, a batter is out leg before wicket if:

1. **The ball hits the batter's body** without first hitting the bat
2. **The ball would have hit the wicket** if the batter had not been in the way
3. **The ball did not pitch on the leg side** of the wicket

However, there is an important exception: **if the ball strikes the batter outside the line of the off-stump while the batter was attempting to play a stroke, the batter is not out.**

In essence, LBW protects batters from being dismissed when they legitimately attempt to play a shot but miss, while preventing them from using their body as an illegal shield to protect the wicket.

how many players are there in rugby.answer:
# Number of Players in Rugby

The number of players in rugby depends on the format:

**Rugby Union (standard format):** Each team has **15 players** on the field. The game is played over 80 minutes, divided into two 40-minute halves.

**Rugby Sevens:** Each team has **7 players** on the field. Games last 14 minutes (two 7-minute halves), except for finals which can have two 10-minute halves. Teams can nominate five substitutes, but only three may play in any one game.

**Under 19 variations:** Teams have **22 players** available, with at least 6 required to play in the front row.

how many players in rugby vs cricket.answer:
# Player Numbers in Rugby vs Cricket

**Cricket:** A cricket team consists of **11 players**, including a captain. While teams can agree to play more than eleven-a-side outside official competitions, no more than eleven players may field at any time.

**Rugby:** Rugby union is played with **15 players** per team. In rugby sevens (a variant), teams have **7 players** on the field.

So in standard formats, rugby union has 15 players per side compared to cricket's 11 players per side.
